per_entity_results.reference: Performance Evaluation of QUIC Implementations with Limited Bandwidth

per_entity_results.generated: This dataset evaluates multiple QUIC protocol implementations under varying bandwidth limits, measuring energy consumption, transfer time, and goodput. The experiment uses two nodes to run looped server-client benchmarks, synchronizing via pos_sync and capturing per-run logs with SSL key data. Evaluation scripts aggregate logs, validate consistency, and generate boxplots comparing performance across implementations. Artifacts include per-run metrics, configuration scripts, and analysis outputs for reproducible comparative analysis.
per_entity_results.reference: This experiment executes a single file download with client-server pairs of different QUIC implementations. The filesize, the implementations, and a bandwidth limit can be set to multiple values to execute the measurements with all combinations of the parameters. The amount of repetitions can also be set to repeat the same combination n times to ensure stable results. An inbuilt evaluation script aggregates results and creates a simple plot that compares different QUIC implementations.

per_entity_results.generated: This script orchestrates QUIC server benchmarks across implementations, applying configurable bandwidth limits and measuring energy consumption per run. It synchronizes with a client via pos_sync tags, handles per-run log capture including SSL key logs, and uploads results with pos_upload. The looped role includes setup, workload execution, and cleanup with checksum verification of test files.
per_entity_results.reference: Start a QUIC server with a given library. In advance, a testfile and a certificate is generated. If set, a bandwidth limit is applied with Linux tc. After a client signalizes a finished transfer, the server is terminated.

per_entity_results.generated: Each iteration executes a parameter sweep over implementation, filesize, and bandwidth_limit, orchestrating server setup, workload transfer with synchronization, timing and checksum capture, and per-run log uploads via pos_sync and pos_upload.
per_entity_results.reference: In each iteration, the QUIC client downloads a testfile from the QUIC server. All combinations for all values of implementation, bandwidth and filesize are run at least once, depending on the amount of repetitions set. For each run, the duration as well as logs from the respective endpoint are stored.
